title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch signals IPO readiness as AI agents fuel revenue surge
description: "The company is ready and getting more ready for every day," Rauch said about an IPO at HumanX conference.

title: Slate Auto raises $650 million as production gets closer and closer
description: The Slate Truck will start in the "mid-$20,000s" when it goes on sale in late 2026.

title: Meta spins up AI version of Mark Zuckerberg to engage with employees
description: The Meta chief is personally involved in training and testing his animated AI.

title: Want to understand the current state of AI? Check out these charts.
description: If you’re following AI news, you’re probably getting whiplash. AI is a gold rush. AI is a bubble. AI is taking your job. AI can’t even read a clock. title: The Download: how humans make decisions, and Moderna’s “vaccine” word games
description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. title: To teach in the time of ChatGPT is to know pain
description: LLM use is the most demoralizing problem I’ve faced as a college instructor.

title: The Internet's Most Powerful Archiving Tool Is in Peril
description: As major news outlets cut off the Wayback Machine, journalists and advocacy groups are rallying to protect the Internet Archive’s vast collection of web pages.

title: Job titles of the future: Wildlife first responder
description: Grizzly bears have made such a comeback across eastern Montana that in 2017, the state hired its first-ever prairie-based grizzly manager: wildlife biologist Wesley Sarmento.  For some seven years, Sarmento worked to keep both the bears, which are still listed as threatened under the Endangered

title: You have no choice in reading this article—maybe
description: Uri Maoz loved doing his human research, back when he was getting his PhD. title: AI Agents Are Coming for Your Dating Life
description: The developers of Pixel Societies are using AI agents to simulate social interactions. It's an attempt optimize the process of choosing new colleagues, friends, and even romantic partners.

title: The largest orbital compute cluster is open for business
description: Kepler Communications is flying 40 GPUs in Earth orbit. And its latest customer is Sophia Space.

title: Trump officials may be encouraging banks to test Anthropic’s Mythos model
description: The report is particularly surprising since the Department of Defense recently declared Anthropic a supply-chain risk.

title: Apple reportedly testing four designs for upcoming smart glasses
description: These glasses are a step back from an ambitious plan that once called for Apple to launch a variety of mixed and augmented reality devices.

title: From LLMs to hallucinations, here’s a simple guide to common AI terms
description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

title: Shock from Iran war has Trump's vision for US energy dominance flailing
description: Record domestic oil and gas production hasn't saved US drivers from price spikes.

title: AI models are terrible at betting on soccer—especially xAI Grok
description: Systems from Google, OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI struggle with the Premier League.

title: Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI
description: Plus: Iran’s internet blackout hits the 1,000-hour mark, cryptocurrency scams result in a record amount of money stolen from Americans, and more.

title: How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors
description: From AI-generated images to restricted satellite data, the systems used to verify what’s real online are struggling to keep up.

title: Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think
description: The new AI model is being heralded—and feared—as a hacker’s superweapon. Experts say its arrival is a wake-up call for developers who have long made security an afterthought.

title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
description: Is it the Department of Defense or the Department of War? The Gulf of Mexico or the Gulf of America? A vaccine—or an “individualized neoantigen treatment”? title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
